---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
html {
background-color: grey;
}

body {
width: 1200px;
box-shadow: 0 0 24px 5px;
margin: auto;
background-color: #FFF;
padding: 24px 32px;
}

h2 {
font-size: 40px;
text-align: center;
width: 800px;
margin: auto;
}

p {
font-size: 24px;
}
```

## Exploring the impact of the type of keyboard on typing performance

This report aims to analyse how the type of keyboard has an effect on typing performance, specifically on the wpm and accuracy of typing tests. 

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Loading the csv data
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQaDzheAaXF5a9Cf6sbnulXHtZlRLIaksuYAfhwZUM11gKalE0kH5rWjr_EphJrDWLkTUq6x9Ui58Pl/pub?output=csv") 

# Renaming the relevant variables
# The mutate function is used to shorten the name for the variable 'keyboard' so that it doesn't display the word 'keyboard' on every entry
latest_data <- rename(
  logged_data,
  keyboard = 2,
  wpm = 3,
  acc = 4
) %>% mutate(keyboard = str_remove(keyboard, " keyboard"))

# The two summary values chosen for the report
acc_mean <- mean(latest_data$acc)
wpm_mean <- mean(latest_data$wpm)
# The two bar charts chosen, with labels
latest_data %>% ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = wpm, fill = keyboard)) +
  labs(title = "Keyboard impact on words per minute (wpm)",
       subtitle = "Comparying the wpm achieved on a typing test to the type of keyboard used",
       x = "Test wpm",
       y = "Number of tests",
       fill = "Keyboard type")

latest_data %>% ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = acc, fill = keyboard)) +
  labs(title = "Keyboard impact on accuracy",
       subtitle = "Comparing the accuracy of a typing test to the type of keyboard used",
       x = "Test accuracy",
       y = "Number of tests",
       fill = "Keyboard type")

```

`r paste("The average accuracy across all the tests is", acc_mean)`

`r paste("The average wpm across all the tests is", wpm_mean)`

![](https://c.tenor.com/-qBsG1HwR4oAAAAC/tenor.gif)
